seed: restlessness vs peace; memory_vs_motion; diagonal_argument; edge_crawl; flame against shatter; closed_corners; light_vs_dark; patch; signal_noise | abstract variation request 84; target batch count 500
title: Overflow vs Containment
reasoning: The emotional tone contrasts restlessness with peace, manifesting as a struggle between chaotic movement and tranquil clarity. I selected a palette of clean white, ice blue, ink black, violet gray, and warning orange to represent the dynamic interplay between calm and tension. The use of warm orange serves as a highlight that disrupts the cooler, more subdued colors, creating a collision in the center of the composition, where the most intense colors are placed. The layout features two pulsating centers that signify the push and pull of conflicting emotions, while broken pixels add intentional disruptions, mirroring the nature of memories and the sensation of motion. The pattern of alternating checker and ladder designs illustrates the stark contrast between serenity and anxiety, leading to a complex dialogue through color and form.

layout.name: two_pulses
layout.description: Two small centers pulse toward or away from each other across negative space.

composition.name: overflow_vs_containment
composition.idea: Bright or hot marks exceed a boundary while cooler marks try to contain them.

referenceExample.title: Mourning Opens
referenceExample.pattern: dark veil splits where green and pale yellow marks climb through

generationRules: Pick 1 pattern type for emotion A.
Pick 1 pattern type for emotion B.
Pick 1 shape seed as the central object.
Apply 1 to 6 broken pixels to create imperfection.
Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the mood is panic, rage, urgency, euphoria, or pressure.
Use the shape seed as an abstract organizing metaphor, not as a literal icon.
Make the two selected pattern types visibly collide, overlap, interrupt, or trade space.
Prefer 4 to 8 distinct hex colors, with at least one quiet field color and one accent color.
Keep the 8x8 grid readable at thumbnail size by preserving clear large-scale structure.
Let one emotion dominate 55 to 70 percent of the square unless the selected layout demands balance.
Use broken pixels as intentional disruptions, scars, repairs, leaks, or false echoes.
Avoid perfectly even distribution unless the chosen composition is control, serenity, clarity, or certainty.
Give the main collision a location: center, edge, corner, diagonal, border, or channel.
generationSpec.negativeSpaceRule: Leave at least 10 percent negative space unless the chosen emotional tone needs dense pressure.
